purpose: Expose wishlist behavior through an authenticated, ownership-safe Store boundary.
success_outcome: Authenticated customers can list/add/remove only their own favorites, while guest and cross-customer attempts fail without leakage.
anti_goals: Do not implement authentication providers/session creation or a new production bearer path.
Do not accept customer ID or record owner from client.

success_checks: Routes use standard customer middleware and actor-derived ownership; production storefront remains session-cookie only.
List/add share exact item projection; four hidden cases use one non-disclosing 404 and out-of-stock remains visible.
Guest and two-customer attempts create no state/disclosure; synthetic bearer is confined to the existing local harness.
evidence_required: Guest and production-style session output plus existing local synthetic-bearer harness evidence where needed.
Exact projection, unified hidden 404, out-of-stock, and ownership assertions.
Typecheck, lint, T3 human checkpoint, and recovery note.

stop_conditions: Authenticated customer actor is unavailable through standard Medusa middleware.
Ownership cannot be enforced without client-supplied customer ID.
Exact item projection or unified hidden 404 cannot be preserved.
Implementation would add a production bearer mechanism or expose cross-customer/sensitive data.
required_handoff: Report changed_files, commands_run, evidence, scope_compliance, and blockers_or_none.
Run /verify and /red-verify; record HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and ROLLBACK_RECOVERY_NOTE before T3 closure.
